sutta: and 70 1 the buddha said monks where there is bodily action there arises to the self pleasure or pain caused by intention of bodily action amongst where there is verbal action there arises pleasure or pain to the self caused by intentional action of speech or monks where there is mental action there arises pleasure or pain to the self caused by intentional action of mind or it is due to ignorance monks it is due to ignorance that either of himself 1 wills bodily intentions falling on which arises to the self that pleasure or pain or others will bodily intentions against him falling on which arises to the self that pleasure or pain either deliberately amongst 1 wills bodily intentions falling on which arises to the self that pleasure or pain or unwittingly does he will bodily intention either of himself monks 1 wills verbal intentions falling on which arises to the cell that pleasure or pain or others will verbal intention against him falling on which arises pleasure or pain either deliberately monks 1 will's verbal intentions or unwittingly does he real verbal intentions again monks either of himself 1 wills mental intentions falling on which arises to the self that pleasure or pain or others will mental intentions against him falling on which arises pleasure or pain either deliberately amongst 1 will's mental intentions or unwittingly does he will mental intention monks in these instances ignorance is followed but by the utter seizing and ending of ignorance that bodily intention exists not falling on which that pleasure or pain arises to the cell that verbal intention that mental intention exists not following on which that pleasure or pain arises to the self there is no feel no base no sphere of action no occasion following on which that pleasure or pain arises to the cell and this uh is 1 of the sutas about karma and the pali word here is which means will's bodily intentions the second 1 is which means will's verbal intentions the third 1 is which means wheels mental intentions and we note here that in all these instances of karma the third 1 is and it is not chitta sankara in the patica samupada dependent origination the sankharas are mentioned as kaya sankara sankara and chitta sankara and there are 2 sets of sankharas we find in the sutas 1 is always used when it concerns kama just like this concerns gamma and we use manu sankara the third 1 whereas in particular dependent origination it is chitta sankara and chitta sankara we find has to not has to do not with kama but with this cessation association uh if you are interested you can look at my book dependent origination index explains the difference between these 2 sets of sankaras and we find it towards the end that when ignorance is ended with the seizing and ending of ignorance there is no more karma created 4.18.100 and 70 2 monks there are these 4 ways of getting personality what for there is monks getting a personality in the getting of which 1's own intention not that of another person has effect there is that in the getting of which another person's intention has effect not 1's own there is that in which both 1's own and another's intention have effect and there is that in which neither has effect these are made for when this was said the venerable sariputta said this to the exalted 1 of this law that has been uttered by the exalted 1 in brief i understand the meaning in detail in the case where there is getting of personality in which 1's own intention not another's has effect there is disease there is disease for those beings from that group because of 1's own intention in the case where there is getting a personality in which another's intention not not 1's own has effect there is disease for those beings from that group because of another's intention in the case where there is getting a personality in which both have effect there is disease for those beings from that group because of the intention both of oneself and and of another but lord in the case where there is getting of personality in which neither has effect what sort of devas are to be regarded therein and the buddha said in that case it is the devas who have attained the realm in which is neither perception nor non-perception and sariputta said pray lord what is the reason what is the cause why certain beings deceasing from that group are returners who come back to this state of things again lord what is the reason what is the cause why certain beings deceasing from that group are non-returners who come not back to this state of things and the buddha said in this first case sariputta in a certain person the fatters that bind to this world are not abandoned such a 1 in this very life attains the realm where there is neither perception or non-perception and abides therein he enjoys his sweetness longs for it finds happiness therein established therein given there to generally spending his time there in not falling away there from when he makes an end he is reborn in that company when he deceives when he diseases then he is a returner he comes back to this state of things in the other case sariputta in a certain person those factors are abandoned such a 1 in this very life attains the realm where there is neither perception or non-perception he enjoys his sweetness longs for it etc when he makes an end he is reborn in that company but deceasing then as he is a non-returner he comes not back to this state of things this is the reason this is the cause sariputta why certain beings are non-returners who come not back to this state of things the thing about this suta which i like to point out is that it is mentioned here that there are certain persons because of their cultivation of the arupa jana the base of neither perception nor non-perception and they abide in it constantly and they enjoy that state and constantly spend their time in that state when they pass away and they are reborn in that arupa jana realm but there are 2 types of person 1 is he has destroyed those factors and he does not come back to [Music] the human world whereas there's another 1 who has not destroyed the factors he comes back to the human uh birth again so from here you find the 1 that does not come back to the human realm again he's a non-returner and anagami so this is 1 of the few sutasa which shows that an anagami can be reborn in the plain of neither perception or non-perception usually anagamis are reborn in the sudavasa abode which is the fourth jhana plane but in this suta it shows that they can also be reborn in the arupa jana plain now we come to another suta which is also quite a very important suita also
